description: MCP server registry. Subagents must use the unified /api/mcp/* routes to talk to these servers — direct connections bypass governance.

servers.name: Supr Internal Tools
servers.description: Built-in Supr tool registry (shell, web_search, project_flow, web_scrape, etc.). Always available.

servers.name: Supr Skill Catalog
servers.description: Structured skill catalog under .agents/skills/. Each skill exposes a trigger list and a system-prompt override.

servers.name: Supr Memory Bank
servers.description: Long-term memory sections and per-mission memory items. Read/write gated by Draft tier.

servers.name: Supr Composio Bridge
servers.description: Bridges to third-party SaaS apps (GitHub, Slack, Notion, Gmail, etc.) via the Composio API. The composioBridge resolves the API key from Settings on every call.

servers.name: GitHub
servers.description: Standard MCP server for GitHub. Pulls repos, creates issues, and manages task branches. Requires a PAT.

servers.name: PostgreSQL
servers.description: Standard MCP server for PostgreSQL. Lets agents query the database schema and run read-only SQL. Requires a connection string.

servers.name: Filesystem
servers.description: Governed file-system surface for the Code Agent. Rooted at $SUPR_FILESYSTEM_ROOT (default: /workspace). Operators can override the root by setting the env var before launching Supr.

servers.name: Brave Search
servers.description: Standard MCP server for Brave Search. Lets agents run web queries. Requires a BRAVE_API_KEY.

servers.name: Context7 (HTTP example)
servers.description: Reference HTTP transport example. Context7 serves up-to-date library docs. Operators can override the URL via CONTEXT7_MCP_URL and supply CONTEXT7_API_KEY for auth.
